description: One-pass review of crate secretx-desktop (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-desktop). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one P0 bug (Linux daemon-absent path) and one P1 (silent mock fallback on unsupported platforms) are real correctness issues that a user will hit. Otherwise the surface area is small and the URI parsing is well-fenced.
Correctness confidence: ~80% chance of no missed bug. Least confident flags: (a) the exact dbus-secret-service error variant produced when daemon is absent — verified via decode_error in keyring source, but not by running it; (b) cancel-safety analysis assumes spawn_blocking continues running on JoinHandle drop, which is the documented tokio behavior. Uncertainty: did not run the actual Secret Service / macOS / Windows code paths; no integration test to exercise; behavior on iOS/Android with mock fallback inferred from keyring source, not observed.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — no idiom red flags. Missing #[derive(Debug)] is the only mandatory fix. Error-mapping duplication is a maintainability tax that grows when issue 1 is fixed (PlatformFailure mapping) — fix it before that fix lands.
Rust idiom confidence: ~90% chance of no missed issue. Least confident flags: opinion-grade Box<str> suggestion (filed with 'opinion' label). Uncertainty: did not check whether the workspace has internal style conventions about Debug derives that I should follow; checked secretx-keyring as a reference and it also lacks #[derive(Debug)] — so this is a workspace-wide gap, but still worth fixing.
title: Review: secretx-desktop rusty pass
